title: Energy Trends and Prices statistical release: 27 May 2021
body: "Energy production and consumption statistics are provided in total and by fuel and provide an analysis of the latest 3 months data compared to the same period a year earlier. Energy price statistics cover domestic price indices, prices of road fuels and petroleum products and comparisons of international road fuel prices.  Energy production and consumption  Highlights for the 3 month period January 2021 to March 2021, compared to the same period a year earlier include:   Primary energy consumption in the UK on a fuel input basis fell by 5.4% with a noticeable reduction in petroleum consumption as demand for transport fuels fell due to the Covid-19 pandemic lockdowns. On a temperature adjusted basis consumption fell by 9.5%. (table ET 1.2) and (table ET 3.13) Indigenous energy production fell by 11% due to maintenance activities and less favourable weather conditions for renewable technologies. (table ET 1.1) Electricity generation by Major Power Producers down 2.4%, with coal down 30%, nuclear down 12% due to outages and renewables down 15% due to less favourable weather conditions, but gas up 22% to meet shortfall.* (table ET 5.4) Gas provided 42.3% of electricity generation by Major Power Producers, with renewables at 38.3%, nuclear at 15.4% and coal at 3.2%.* (table ET 5.4) Low carbon share of electricity generation by Major Power Producers down 7.2  percentage points to 53.7%, whilst fossil fuel share of electricity generation stood at 45.8%.* (table ET 5.4)   *Major Power Producers (MPPs) data published monthly, all generating companies data published quarterly.  Energy prices  Highlights for May 2021 compared to April 2021:   Petrol and diesel prices rose by 1.7 and 1.8 pence per litre respectively. (table QEP 4.1.1)   Contacts Lead statistician Warren Evans, Tel 0300 068 5059  Press enquiries, Tel 020 7215 1000  Data periods and coverage  Statistics on monthly production and consumption of coal, electricity, gas, oil and total energy include data for the UK for the period up to the end of March 2021.  Statistics on average temperatures, wind speeds, sun hours and rainfall include data for the UK for the period up to the end of April 2021.  Statistics on energy prices include retail price data for the UK for April 2021, and petrol & diesel data for May 2021, with EU comparative data for April 2021.  Next release  The next release of provisional monthly energy statistics will take place on Tuesday 29 June 2021.  Data tables  To access the data tables associated with this release please click on the relevant subject link(s) below. For further information please use the contact details provided.  Please note that the links below will always direct you to the latest data tables. If you are interested in historical data tables please contact BEIS (kevin.harris@beis.gov.uk)     Subject and table number Energy production and consumption, and weather data     Total Energy taxons.title: Energy and climate change: evidence and analysis
